rules.name: Heading Alignment and Integrity
rules.query: Examine title lines and subtitle lines in the page heading area outside tables, using extracted text plus any available positional metadata. Determine whether they appear complete and aligned consistently with the other related heading lines. The intended pattern may be centered, left-aligned, or right-aligned. Treat natural PDF line wrapping of long titles or subtitles as acceptable, including cases where a long name or phrase continues on the next line in a way that may sound awkward when flattened into plain text. Express feedback in plain language rather than raw coordinates or measurements.
rules.description: Checks heading completeness and consistent alignment across title/subtitle lines.
rules.acceptance_criteria: Title lines and subtitle lines in the page heading area should be complete, readable, and aligned consistently with their peer heading lines based on extracted text and any available line-position metadata. Do not flag a long title or subtitle merely because it continues onto the next line. Read adjacent heading lines together as a combined heading, and allow normal PDF wrapping even when the flattened text sounds awkward, repetitive, or stylistically incomplete. Do not fail this rule for wording preference, naming style, or because you think a wrapped entity name should have been repeated differently. Only report a content-integrity issue when there is clear evidence of visible truncation, cut-off text, corrupted extraction, or a genuinely malformed heading after considering natural line continuation. Only describe a line as off-center when the dominant heading pattern is actually centered. Report all distinct issues for this rule on the page. Use needs_review when the extraction or layout metadata is insufficient or when the available positional metadata does not cover the candidate line.

rules.name: Whole-Dollar Values Check
rules.query: Confirm all amounts shown inside tables or chart-like numeric blocks are rounded to whole dollars. No cents or decimal dollar values should appear. Ignore percentages and footnotes. A single page may contain multiple separate tables.
rules.description: All dollar amounts in tables and charts must be whole dollars. No cents, no decimal values. Ignore percentages and footnotes.
rules.acceptance_criteria: No decimal numbers are used on dollar amounts or dollar figures presented in tables or chart-like numeric sections. These must be whole numbers. Ignore percentages. Report all distinct issues for this rule on the page.

rules.name: Dollar Sign Placement
rules.query: Evaluate each relevant table separately. Determine the first monetary row and the last monetary row in each table, ignoring headers, blank rows, percentage-only rows, and other non-monetary rows. Only those two boundary rows are required to show a dollar sign ($). Interior rows may include or omit dollar signs and should not drive the verdict.
rules.description: Dollar signs are required only on the first and last monetary row of each table; interior rows are not checked.
rules.acceptance_criteria: For each relevant table, the first monetary row and the last monetary row should contain a dollar sign. Do not fail the rule because an interior or penultimate row lacks a dollar sign. If the extraction makes the table boundary or the monetary boundary rows ambiguous, use needs_review. Report all distinct issues for this rule on the page.

rules.name: Visual Layout Integrity
rules.query: Review the full page layout including headings, tables, columns, logos, and overall visual alignment. All visually relevant page elements should be visually complete, aligned, uniformly rendered, and free of cut-off words, misprints, errant spacing, deformation, or column misalignment. Use the red center guideline as a supporting visual cue when helpful.
rules.description: Visual completeness and layout integrity of all page elements: headings, tables, columns, logos.
rules.acceptance_criteria: The page layout is visually clean and consistent. Headings, tables, columns, and logos are aligned and visually complete. No text is cut off, misprinted, deformed, or obviously misaligned. Report all distinct issues for this rule on the page.

rules.name: Refoot Column Totals
rules.query:
Recalculate all column totals; down/across must match.

Pass criteria: Totals match exactly (tolerance 0).

Fail criteria: Any mismatch ≥ $1.
rules.description: Recalculate all column totals; down/across must match.
rules.acceptance_criteria: Totals match exactly (tolerance 0).

rules.name: Cross-foot Subtotals
rules.query:
Cross-foot where totals derive from multiple subtotals.

Pass criteria: All cross-foot relationships hold.

Fail criteria: Any mismatch ≥ $1.
rules.description: Cross-foot where totals derive from multiple subtotals.
rules.acceptance_criteria: All cross-foot relationships hold.

rules.name: Signage Consistency
rules.query:
Signage per policy. Liabilities may be shown as positive; only flag inconsistent signage within the same statement. Losses/expenses should be negative (parentheses or minus) per policy.

Pass criteria: No mixed signage for the same class of items within a statement; assets and totals not negative; losses/expenses carry negative sign (paren or minus).

Fail criteria: Mixed signage in the same liabilities or expense column; negative assets or totals.
rules.description: Signage per policy. Liabilities may be shown as positive; only flag inconsistent signage within the same statement. Losses/expenses should be negative (parentheses or minus) per policy.
rules.acceptance_criteria: No mixed signage for the same class of items within a statement; assets and totals not negative; losses/expenses carry negative sign (paren or minus).

rules.name: Font & Capitalization Consistency
rules.query:
Check font and capitalization consistency across the page. All text of the same type (headers, line items, totals) should use consistent fonts and capitalization patterns. Do NOT check alignment — that is handled by a separate rule (FMT-HEADINGS). Do NOT check double-underlines — that is handled by a separate vision rule. Focus only on: (1) font consistency, (2) capitalization patterns.

Pass criteria: Fonts and capitalization are internally consistent across the page.

Fail criteria: Mixed fonts or inconsistent capitalization for the same class of content.
rules.description: Font and capitalization consistency across the page. Alignment and double-underlines are checked by separate rules.
rules.acceptance_criteria: Fonts and capitalization are internally consistent across the page.

rules.name: Double Underline
rules.query:
Final section totals are double-underlined. A "final section total" is the last and most comprehensive figure in a discrete section or statement. A page may contain multiple final section totals if it contains multiple sections, each of which should be double-underlined independently. A "section" is a group of line items introduced by a bold heading; subsections within it may have non-bold headings and their totals are intermediate, not final.

Intermediate subtotals are NOT required to be double-underlined and should be ignored by this rule.

How to identify a final section total vs. an intermediate subtotal:
- A final section total is not further added into another subtotal on the same page.
- An intermediate subtotal feeds into a higher-level total that also appears on the same page.

Look for TWO distinct parallel horizontal lines beneath each final section total row — not a single line, not a thick rule, but two separate lines with a visible gap between them.

Pass criteria: You can clearly identify two separate parallel lines beneath the final section total figure.

Fail criteria: You can clearly and confidently see only one underline line with no second line present — and you are certain it is not a thick double underline rendered at close spacing — on a row that qualifies as a final section total.

Ambiguous cases: In rasterized PDFs, double underlines may appear as a single thick line when the two lines are printed close together. If you are uncertain whether you see one thick line or two close lines, return `needs_review` — do not default to `fail`.

If vector metadata is provided in the prompt (labeled VECTOR DATA), treat it as authoritative and do not override it with visual judgment alone.
rules.description: Final statement totals are double-underlined.
rules.acceptance_criteria: Two distinct parallel lines clearly visible on each total row, OR `needs_review` when visual ambiguity prevents a confident determination.

rules.name: Date Validity
rules.query:
Check any dates visible on this page: Issue Date, Approval Date, Signature Date, Period End, Wire Date. These dates are OPTIONAL — do not fail if a date type is absent. But if a date IS present, it must be a real calendar date (not February 30, not month 13, etc.). Normalize common formats: "MMMM D, YYYY", "YYYY-MM-DD", "M/D/YYYY". If the page contains no dates at all, return not_applicable.

Pass criteria: All dates present on the page are valid calendar dates.

Fail criteria: Any present date is impossible or clearly a typo.
rules.description: Validate Issue, Signature, Period End (and Wire if applicable) are real calendar dates; Issue/Approval/Signature are OPTIONAL, but if present they must be valid.
rules.acceptance_criteria: All present dates are valid; optional dates may be absent without causing a fail.

rules.name: Date Logical Order
rules.query:
Check date ordering on this page ONLY when both dates in a pair are present. Pairs to check: Issue Date >= Period End; Wire Date >= Issue Date; Signature Date >= Issue Date. If only one date in a pair is present, skip that pair (no finding). If the page contains fewer than two dates, return not_applicable.

Pass criteria: All evaluated pairs obey logical order; pairs with a missing member are skipped.

Fail criteria: Any evaluated pair is out of order.
rules.description: Date ordering checks apply ONLY when both dates in a pair are present (Issue ≥ Period End; Wire ≥ Issue; Signature ≥ Issue).
rules.acceptance_criteria: All evaluated pairs obey logical order; pairs with a missing member are skipped (no finding).

rules.name: Grammar & Spelling
rules.query:
Grammar and Spelling Consistency (major) with case whitelist for “Unaudited/UNAUDITED”.

Pass criteria: No unwhitelisted spelling/grammar findings.

Fail criteria: ≥1 unwhitelisted issue.
rules.description: Grammar and Spelling Consistency (major) with case whitelist for “Unaudited/UNAUDITED”.
rules.acceptance_criteria: No unwhitelisted spelling/grammar findings.

rules.name: Cover Period Phrasing
rules.query:
This rule applies ONLY to the cover page. If this page is clearly not a cover page, return not_applicable.

Accepted cover phrasings include: "As of {date}", "For the Period Ended {date}", "As of and for the Period Ended {date}", and "For the Year Ended {date}".

Pass criteria: Cover uses one of the allowed phrasings with a valid date.

Fail criteria: Phrasing does not match any of the allowed patterns, or the date is invalid.
rules.description: Accepted cover phrasings include: “As of {date}”, “For the Period Ended {date}”, “As of and for the Period Ended {date}”, and “For the Year Ended {date}”.
rules.acceptance_criteria: Cover uses one of the allowed phrasings with a valid {date}.

rules.name: Cover Date Validation
rules.query:
This rule applies ONLY to the cover page. If this page is clearly not a cover page, return not_applicable.

Require a valid Period End date on the cover. The date must be a real calendar date (e.g., December 31, 2024 — not February 30 or other impossible dates). Do not apply date ordering logic on the cover.

Pass criteria: Period End appears on the cover and is a valid calendar date.

Fail criteria: Period End is missing or is an invalid/impossible date.
rules.description: On the cover, require a valid Period End date. Do not apply ordering logic on the cover; validate ordering elsewhere only when both dates exist.
rules.acceptance_criteria: Period End appears on the cover and is a valid date.

rules.name: Balance Sheet Formatting
rules.query:
This rule applies ONLY to the Balance Sheet. If this page is not a Balance Sheet, return not_applicable.

Check Balance Sheet-specific formatting: (1) capitalization is consistent across line items, (2) totals foot correctly (vertical sums match labeled totals), (3) indentation hierarchy is consistent (subtotals indented relative to details). Do NOT check alignment style, dollar sign placement, double-underlines, or rounding — those are handled by separate rules.

Pass criteria: Capitalization consistent; totals foot; indentation logical.

Fail criteria: Capitalization inconsistencies; totals do not foot; indentation breaks hierarchy.
rules.description: Balance Sheet-specific formatting: capitalization, totals footing, indentation. Alignment, $, underlines, and rounding are checked by other rules.
rules.acceptance_criteria: Capitalization consistent; totals foot; indentation logical.

rules.name: Balance Sheet Equation
rules.query:
This rule applies ONLY to the Balance Sheet. If this page is not a Balance Sheet, return not_applicable.

Total Assets must equal Total Liabilities plus Members'/Partners' Capital. Use the labeled final totals only (ignore subtotals). Look for rows labeled "Total assets" and "Total liabilities and members' capital" (or "Total liabilities and partners' capital"). Normalize number formats: remove "$", commas, treat parentheses as negative.

Pass criteria: Assets total = Liabilities + Capital total, tolerance 0.

Fail criteria: Mismatch >= $1.
rules.description: Total Assets must equal Total Liabilities plus Members'/Partners' Capital, using the labeled final totals (ignore subtotals).
rules.acceptance_criteria: Assets total = Liabilities+Capital total, tolerance 0.

rules.name: SOI Formatting
rules.query:
This rule applies ONLY to the Schedule of Investments. If this page is not a Schedule of Investments, return not_applicable.

Check: (1) totals foot correctly, (2) pluralization of "Investment" vs "Investments" matches the actual count of investments listed, (3) formatting is internally consistent. Do NOT check alignment style, dollar signs, or rounding — those are handled by separate rules.

Pass criteria: Totals foot; pluralization correct; formatting consistent.

Fail criteria: Totals off; wrong pluralization; formatting inconsistencies.
rules.description: SOI formatting: totals footing, Investment/Investments pluralization, consistency.
rules.acceptance_criteria: Totals foot; pluralization correct; formatting consistent.

rules.name: SOI Footnote Cross-References
rules.query:
This rule applies ONLY to the Schedule of Investments. If this page is not a Schedule of Investments, return not_applicable.

Check that footnote markers or superscripts (e.g., "(a)", "(1)", "*") appearing next to investment line items have corresponding legend entries on the same page (typically at the bottom). Also check that legend entries reference markers that actually appear in the table above. Flag markers with no matching legend, or legend entries with no corresponding marker.

Pass criteria: All markers have matching legend entries on the same page, and vice versa.

Fail criteria: Any orphaned marker or orphaned legend entry.
rules.description: Check that SOI footnote markers and legend entries match on the same page.
rules.acceptance_criteria: All markers have matching legend entries on the same page, and vice versa.

rules.name: Operations Formatting
rules.query:
This rule applies ONLY to the Statement of Operations (or Statement of Income). If this page is not a Statement of Operations, return not_applicable.

Check: (1) totals foot correctly (column sums match labeled totals), (2) rounding follows whole-dollar policy (no cents), (3) capitalization and indentation are consistent. Do NOT check signage — that is handled by a separate rule (SIG-GLOBAL). Do NOT check alignment style or double-underlines.

Pass criteria: Totals foot; rounding correct; formatting consistent.

Fail criteria: Totals do not foot; cents present; formatting inconsistencies.
rules.description: Operations statement formatting: totals footing, rounding, capitalization. Signage and alignment are checked by other rules.
rules.acceptance_criteria: Totals foot; rounding correct; formatting consistent.

rules.name: Cash Flow Signage
rules.query:
This rule applies ONLY to the Statement of Cash Flows. If this page is not a Statement of Cash Flows, return not_applicable.

Check that cash flow signs are logically consistent: cash inflows (proceeds, contributions, income received) should be positive; cash outflows (payments, distributions, expenses paid) should be negative (shown in parentheses or with a minus sign). Flag any logical inversion where an outflow is shown as positive or an inflow as negative. Internal consistency is key — if the statement uses a different sign convention consistently, that is acceptable.

Pass criteria: All signs are logically consistent with cash flow direction.

Fail criteria: Any logical sign inversion (outflow positive or inflow negative) without consistent convention.
rules.description: Cash flow signage: inflows positive, outflows negative (parentheses/minus). Flag logical inversions.
rules.acceptance_criteria: All signs are logically consistent with cash flow direction.

rules.name: Cash Reconciliation
rules.query:
This rule applies ONLY to the Statement of Cash Flows. If this page is not a Statement of Cash Flows, return not_applicable.

Check that: beginning cash + net change in cash = ending cash. Look for labeled rows like "Cash, beginning of period", "Net increase/decrease in cash", and "Cash, end of period" (or similar variations). No disclosure of non-cash activity is required.

Pass criteria: The equation 'beginning + net change = ending' holds exactly.

Fail criteria: Mismatch >= $1.
rules.description: Net change + beginning = ending cash. No disclosure of non-cash activity required.
rules.acceptance_criteria: Equation holds exactly.
